content.problemStatement:
You are given an undirected graph with NNN nodes (numbered 111 through NNN) and MMM edges. Each node has a value; let's denote the value of node iii by AiA_iAi​. Note that the graph is not necessarily simple.

A doubly increasing path is a directed path such that both the values of nodes and differences of values of nodes on this path are strictly increasing. Formally, it is a sequence of nodes p1,p2,p3,…,pkp_1, p_2, p_3, \ldots, p_kp1​,p2​,p3​,…,pk​ for some integer (the length of this path) k≥1k \ge 1k≥1 such that 0<Ap2−Ap1<Ap3−Ap2<…<Apk−Apk−10 \lt A_{p_2} - A_{p_1} \lt A_{p_3} - A_{p_2} \lt \ldots \lt A_{p_k} - A_{p_{k-1}}0<Ap2​​−Ap1​​<Ap3​​−Ap2​​<…<Apk​​−Apk−1​​ and for each valid iii, nodes pip_ipi​ and pi−1p_{i-1}pi−1​ are connected by an edge.

You need to find the length of the longest doubly increasing path in the graph.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.
Each of the following MMM lines contains two space-separated integers uuu and vvv denoting that nodes uuu and vvv are connected by an edge.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer — the maximum length of a doubly increasing path.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: Any path with length 222 from a smaller node to a larger one is doubly increasing. There is no doubly increasing path with length greater than 222.
Example case 2: The path 1→2→51 \rightarrow 2 \rightarrow 51→2→5 is doubly increasing.
